<center>

<h1>

DASC 460: Analyzing the Spatial Spread of Violence in Saint Paul to Determine the Effectiveness of Establishing a Non-Fatal Shooting Unit

</center>

<center>

<h1>

Data Sandbox

</center>

### Load Relevant R Libraries

```{r warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
library(rmarkdown); library(knitr); library(igraph); library(corrplot); library(moments); library(readxl); library(dplyr); library(ggplot2); library(lubridate); library(leaflet); library(readr); library(leaflet.extras2); library(sf); library(janitor); library(broom);library(MASS);

```

#### Load data

```{r}
shootings <- read_excel("Shootings_Full Data(new).xlsx", sheet = "Full Data")
```

#### Processing data

```{r}
model_data <- shootings %>%
  mutate(
    DATE = as.Date(DATE),
    week = floor_date(DATE, unit = "week"),
    trend = as.numeric(week - min(week)) / 7,
    weekend = ifelse(wday(DATE) %in% c(1, 7), 1, 0),
    unit = ifelse(DATE >= as.Date("2024-01-01"), 1, 0)
  ) %>%
  group_by(GRID, week) %>%
  summarise(
    y = n(),
    trend = first(trend),
    weekend = max(weekend),
    unit = first(unit),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```

#### Model 1

```{r}
# -----------------------------------------
# 3. Fit Poisson regression
# -----------------------------------------
fit <- glm(
  y ~ trend + weekend + unit + weekend:unit,
  family = poisson(link = "log"),
  data = model_data
)

# View model summary
summary(fit)

# -----------------------------------------
# 4. Add fitted values and residuals
# -----------------------------------------
model_data <- model_data %>%
  mutate(
    pred = predict(fit, type = "response"),
    resid_pearson = residuals(fit, type = "pearson")
  )

# -----------------------------------------
# 5. Predicted vs observed plot
# -----------------------------------------
ggplot(model_data, aes(x = pred, y = y)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, color = "red") +
  labs(
    title = "Predicted vs Observed",
    x = "Predicted",
    y = "Observed"
  )

# -----------------------------------------
# 6. Residuals vs fitted plot
# -----------------------------------------
ggplot(model_data, aes(x = pred, y = resid_pearson)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "red") +
  labs(
    title = "Residuals vs Fitted",
    x = "Predicted",
    y = "Residuals"
  )

# -----------------------------------------
# 7. Coefficient plot
# -----------------------------------------
coef_df <- tidy(fit)

ggplot(coef_df, aes(x = term, y = estimate)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(
      ymin = estimate - std.error,
      ymax = estimate + std.error
    ),
    width = 0.2
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Poisson Regression Coefficients",
    x = "",
    y = "Estimate (log scale)"
  )

# -----------------------------------------
# 8. Model fit statistics
# -----------------------------------------

# Overdispersion statistic
overdispersion <- sum(residuals(fit, type = "pearson")^2) / fit$df.residual
overdispersion

# Pseudo R-squared
pseudo_r2 <- 1 - (fit$deviance / fit$null.deviance)
pseudo_r2

# AIC
AIC(fit)

# -----------------------------------------
# 9. Exponentiated coefficients
#    These are multiplicative effects
# -----------------------------------------
exp(coef(fit))

# Optional: exponentiated coefficients with confidence intervals
exp(cbind(Estimate = coef(fit), confint(fit)))

# -----------------------------------------
# 10. Optional: simple interpretation table
# -----------------------------------------
interpretation_table <- data.frame(
  term = names(coef(fit)),
  log_estimate = coef(fit),
  exp_estimate = exp(coef(fit)),
  p_value = summary(fit)$coefficients[, 4]
)

interpretation_table
```

#### Model 2

```{r}
all_data <- shootings %>%
  mutate(
    DATE = as.Date(DATE),
    trend = as.numeric(DATE - min(DATE)),
    weekend = ifelse(wday(DATE) %in% c(1,7), 1, 0),
    unit = ifelse(DATE >= as.Date("2024-01-01"), 1, 0)
  )

# Create full grid of all GRID x DATE
full_grid <- expand.grid(
  GRID = unique(all_data$GRID),
  DATE = seq(min(all_data$DATE), max(all_data$DATE), by = "day")
)

# Merge counts
model_data_full <- full_grid %>%
  left_join(all_data %>%
              group_by(GRID, DATE) %>%
              summarise(y = n(), .groups = "drop"),
            by = c("GRID", "DATE")) %>%
  mutate(
    y = ifelse(is.na(y), 0, y),
    trend = as.numeric(DATE - min(DATE)),
    weekend = ifelse(wday(DATE) %in% c(1,7), 1, 0),
    unit = ifelse(DATE >= as.Date("2024-01-01"), 1, 0)
  )
```

```{r}
fit_full <- glm(
  y ~ trend + weekend + unit + weekend:unit,
  family = poisson(link = "log"),
  data = model_data_full
)

fit_spatial <- glm(
  y ~ trend + weekend + unit + weekend:unit + factor(GRID),
  family = poisson(link = "log"),
  data = model_data_full
)

model_data_full <- model_data_full %>%
  arrange(GRID, DATE) %>%
  group_by(GRID) %>%
  mutate(lag_y = lag(y, default = 0)) %>%
  ungroup()

fit_lag <- glm(
  y ~ trend + weekend + unit + weekend:unit + lag_y,
  family = poisson(link = "log"),
  data = model_data_full
)

fit_nb <- glm.nb(
  y ~ trend + weekend + unit + weekend:unit,
  data = model_data_full
)
```

```{r}
model_data$pred <- predict(fit, type = "response")

ggplot(model_data, aes(x = week)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = y), color = "black") +
  geom_line(aes(y = pred), color = "red") +
  labs(title = "Actual vs Predicted Shootings",
       y = "Count")
```

#### Model 3 (Selection)

```{r}

data_full <- shootings %>%
  arrange(DATE, Time) %>%
  mutate(
    DATE = as.Date(DATE),
    trend = as.numeric(DATE - min(DATE)),
    weekend = ifelse(wday(DATE) %in% c(1,7), 1, 0),
    unit = ifelse(DATE >= as.Date("2024-01-01"), 1, 0),
    
    # season
    month = month(DATE),
    season = case_when(
      month %in% c(12,1,2) ~ "winter",
      month %in% c(3,4,5) ~ "spring",
      month %in% c(6,7,8) ~ "summer",
      TRUE ~ "fall"
    ),
    
    # time features
    hour = hour(Time),
    day = wday(DATE, label = TRUE),
    
    # clustering
    time_since_last = as.numeric(difftime(DATE, lag(DATE), units = "days"))
  )
```

```{r}
# DAILY
daily_data <- data_full %>%
  group_by(DATE) %>%
  summarise(
    y = n(),
    trend = first(trend),
    weekend = first(weekend),
    unit = first(unit),
    season = first(season),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# WEEKLY
weekly_data <- data_full %>%
  mutate(week = floor_date(DATE, "week")) %>%
  group_by(week) %>%
  summarise(
    y = n(),
    trend = mean(trend),
    weekend = mean(weekend),
    unit = mean(unit),
    season = first(season),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# MONTHLY
monthly_data <- data_full %>%
  mutate(month = floor_date(DATE, "month")) %>%
  group_by(month) %>%
  summarise(
    y = n(),
    trend = mean(trend),
    weekend = mean(weekend),
    unit = mean(unit),
    season = first(season),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```

```{r}
library(MASS)

run_model_selection <- function(data) {
  
  full_model <- glm(
    y ~ trend + weekend + unit +
        factor(season),
    family = poisson(link = "log"),
    data = data
  )
  
  best_model <- stepAIC(full_model, direction = "both", trace = FALSE)
  
  return(best_model)
}
```

```{r}
model_daily   <- run_model_selection(daily_data)
model_weekly  <- run_model_selection(weekly_data)
model_monthly <- run_model_selection(monthly_data)

AIC(model_daily, model_weekly, model_monthly)
```

```{r}
daily_data <- daily_data %>%
  arrange(DATE) %>%
  mutate(lag_y = lag(y, default = 0))

model_with_lag <- glm(
  y ~ trend + weekend + unit + lag_y + factor(season),
  family = poisson(link = "log"),
  data = daily_data
)

summary(model_with_lag)
AIC(model_with_lag)
```

```{r}
model_interactions <- glm(
  y ~ trend + weekend + unit +
      weekend:unit + season:unit,
  family = poisson(link = "log"),
  data = daily_data
)

AIC(model_interactions)
```

Model Choosing

```{r}

data_full <- shootings %>%
  arrange(DATE, Time) %>%
  mutate(
    DATE = as.Date(DATE),
    trend = as.numeric(DATE - min(DATE)),
    
    # intervention
    unit = ifelse(DATE >= as.Date("2024-01-01"), 1, 0),
    
    # weekend
    weekend = ifelse(wday(DATE) %in% c(1,7), 1, 0),
    
    # season
    month = month(DATE),
    season = case_when(
      month %in% c(12,1,2) ~ "winter",
      month %in% c(3,4,5) ~ "spring",
      month %in% c(6,7,8) ~ "summer",
      TRUE ~ "fall"
    ),
    
    # hour bins
    hour = hour(Time),
    time_bin = case_when(
      hour >= 5 & hour < 12 ~ "morning",
      hour >= 12 & hour < 17 ~ "afternoon",
      hour >= 17 & hour < 21 ~ "evening",
      TRUE ~ "night"
    )
  )
```

```{r}
data_full <- data_full %>%
  arrange(DATE, Time) %>%
  mutate(
    time_since_last = as.numeric(difftime(
      as.POSIXct(paste(DATE, Time)),
      lag(as.POSIXct(paste(DATE, Time))),
      units = "hours"
    ))
  )
```

```{r}
data_full <- data_full %>%
  mutate(
    lat_bin = cut(Lat, breaks = 10),
    lon_bin = cut(Long, breaks = 10),
    location_bin = interaction(lat_bin, lon_bin)
  )
```

```{r}
daily_data <- data_full %>%
  group_by(DATE) %>%
  summarise(
    y = n(),
    trend = first(trend),
    weekend = first(weekend),
    unit = first(unit),
    season = first(season),
    time_bin = names(which.max(table(time_bin))),  # dominant time
    avg_time_since_last = mean(time_since_last, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(DATE) %>%
  mutate(
    lag_y = lag(y, default = 0)
  )
```

```{r}
full_model <- glm(
  y ~ trend + weekend + unit +
      factor(season) +
      factor(time_bin) +
      lag_y +
      avg_time_since_last +
      weekend:unit +
      season:unit,
  family = poisson(link = "log"),
  data = daily_data
)
```

```{r}
best_model <- stepAIC(full_model, direction = "both", trace = FALSE)

summary(best_model)
AIC(best_model)
```

```{r}
daily_data <- data_full %>%
  group_by(DATE) %>%
  summarise(
    y = n(),
    trend = first(trend),
    weekend = first(weekend),
    unit = first(unit),
    season = first(season),
    time_bin = names(which.max(table(time_bin))),
    location_bin = names(which.max(table(location_bin))),  #  add this
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(DATE) %>%
  mutate(
    lag_y = lag(y, default = 0)
  )
```

```{r}
spatial_model <- glm(
  y ~ trend + weekend + unit +
      factor(season) +
      factor(time_bin) +
      lag_y +
      factor(location_bin),
  family = poisson(link = "log"),
  data = daily_data
)

AIC(spatial_model)
```
